items.reason: All 20 decisions (DEC-001 through DEC-020) in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` have id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences (with concrete deletions), and non-empty projects_to. The new platform-shape decisions (DEC-011 through DEC-020) cover privacy surfaces, runtime coverage, audit truth, static mirrors, service-role policy, dictionary direction, canonical fields, global enums, closed privacy classes, and migration direction as required by `decisions-needed.md`.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 (owner Andy, target 2026-05-15, blocker 'Product priority on next runtime slice', rationale on runtime slice scheduling) and PEND-005 (owner Andy, target 2026-05-03, blocker 'CI capacity', rationale on Edge Function type-checking). Both target dates are future relative to the 2026-04-24 research date, both have owners and blockers.

Each decision's consequences section opens with deletion-language bullets naming concrete artifacts. E.g., DEC-001 'Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from `supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts`...'; DEC-007 'Removes duplicate source-ID columns from every public table; `public.source_identifiers` owns...'; DEC-015 'Removes Supabase service-role client construction from `supabase/functions/gradebook-bulk-submit/index.ts`...'. Most decisions also justify preservation with 'Why not collapse:' bullets.

Each decision lists three credible options bracketing the design space. E.g., DEC-001 considers separate-table-per-spec (permissive), collapse-everything-into-person (aggressive), and the chosen middle. DEC-005 includes 'Use AGS line items as the only canonical model', 'Keep all four sources as peers', and the chosen line_item+result+command split — these are real architectures other LMSes ship. DEC-016 includes generate-on-demand vs per-spec-canonical vs shared-canonical-projected.

`data/data-dictionary.seed.json` carries 574 fields across 59 objects projecting all 5 per-spec dictionaries via `spec_dictionary_projections`. `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py --check` is invoked by VERIFY.md and by `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py`, and fails on drift. The seed declares `projection_policy` citing DEC-016: 'data/data-dictionary.seed.json is the upstream dictionary source. The files under dictionary/*.v1.json are generated projections...'.

`data/data-dictionary.seed.json` defines `canonical.identity.person.email` whose `projections` list includes `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email`, `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.email`, `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email`, and `dictionary/qti-core.v1.json#qti_candidate.email` — exactly the four-way email overlap the rubric names. The seed's per-field `canonical_field_id` references on each spec field carry this through to per-spec projections.

`canonical_objects[0]` defines `canonical.identity.person` as a real object with id/display_name/email/source_subject_id/identity_kind fields, allowed values for identity_kind ('known_person'/'external_agent'), and explicit `projections` to OneRoster `person`, Caliper `caliper_actor`, QTI `qti_candidate`, LTI `lti_membership`, and the LTI launch `subject_id` field. The unification is data, not prose.

Each spec dictionary's `shared_enums` carries `shared_enum_id`, `canonical_values`, `crosswalk` (spec-native -> canonical), and `reverse_crosswalk` (canonical -> spec-native). E.g., `grading_progress` in `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json` maps OneRoster `fullyGraded` and LTI AGS `FullyGraded` both to canonical `fully_graded`, with the reverse_crosswalk listing both spec-native values. `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` enforces shared_enum_id presence and crosswalk completeness.

DEC-019 closes the list to nine values: public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system. `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` enforces this with `INVALID_PRIVACY_CLASSES = {'depends_on_contents', 'depends_on_entity'}` and `validate_privacy_class()` which rejects any `depends_on_*` placeholder or value outside the closed list across seed objects, fields, and canonical objects.

DEC-020 declares migrations should be generated from a dictionary relational graph, but `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` is a hand-written 19KB SQL file with foreign keys, indexes, and RLS policies that are nowhere declared in `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` (the seed shows objects with `fields` but no `relationships` array, and no `references`/`cardinality`/`on_delete` per-field metadata). No generator script (e.g., `scripts/generate_migrations.py`) exists in VERIFY.md or the scripts directory, and there is no drift check that would fail if the migration diverged from the dictionary.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: add per-field `relationships` (target_object, target_field, cardinality, ownership, on_delete) for every FK currently in supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql, then add scripts/generate_migrations.py and a --check mode wired into VERIFY.md so committed migrations match the graph.

items.reason: `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` validates that every dictionary field has a `decision_id` matching a decision in the parsed decision-trace block of `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` (`'{config['name']} {field_ref} references unknown decision_id {decision_id}'`). Sampled fields like `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.primary_role` carry `decision_id: DEC-003-role-vocabulary` and the trace block lists that field under DEC-003's `produces_fields`. Allowed values and shared enums also carry `decision_id` references.

Generated dictionary pages exist (`docs/generated/*-dictionary.md`, `site/docs/*-dictionary.html`) and `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` enforces zero-diff between source and generated artifacts. However, `site/index.html` is a hand-edited prose portal containing load-bearing claims (e.g., 'CASE standards and competency graph for alignment...', 'OAuth scopes mapped to dictionary fields') that do not link to specific dictionary anchors — the developer guide section uses static `<ul>` lists with no `href` to dictionary entries. The hosted-API code panels reference Supabase URLs but not the dictionary fields they expose.
items.traced_cause: site/index.html and site/app.js: replace the static guide bullets and code panels with dictionary-linked anchors generated from data/data-dictionary.seed.json (or scripts/build_site_docs.py output) so every load-bearing field, scope, and enum claim resolves to a dictionary entry.

items.reason: `site/index.html` has a `#decisions` section that renders decision cards from the register and `docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html` is linked. However, plain-language 'why' pages tying concrete platform concepts (separate `agent` vs `person`, enrollment vs membership, CASE as canonical) to their decision IDs do not exist as docs the layperson reads — the only explanation is the dense decision register itself. The hero copy and 'Two developer layers' section state what without explaining why those choices were made.
items.traced_cause: site/index.html or new docs/why-*.md pages: write per-concept plain-language pages (why agent vs person, why enrollment vs membership, why CASE is canonical) that summarize each decision's choice and the work it deletes, and link to the decision register anchor instead of relying on the raw register as the only explanation.

items.reason: `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` (or any equivalent) does not exist. README.md lists artifacts but no end-to-end teaching-app guide. The buildability guide must walk through (a) creating a school, (b) enrolling a student, (c) posting a grade, (d) linking to a CASE standard, and (e) reading a Caliper feed with copy-pasteable curl/SQL — none of these flows are documented end-to-end. The only runnable example is a `curl http://localhost:8787/users/USER-ADA` snippet in README.md, which covers only step (a) partially.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: write the five-step layperson guide with copy-pasteable curl against https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1 and the Edge Functions, with every field linking to the corresponding dictionary entry and every enum value linking to the global shared_enum.

items.reason: No link-checker output is evident in VERIFY.md or any test artifact, and the rubric requires zero 404s plus three-click reachability of every dictionary object, decision, and endpoint. The portal does have a `#decisions` section and `docs/index.html`, but with no automated link check there is no evidence that every decision (DEC-001..DEC-020) and every dictionary object across all 5 per-spec dictionaries is reachable from the portal landing within three clicks.
items.traced_cause: VERIFY.md and a new scripts/check_links.py: add a link checker that walks rendered site/ HTML, asserts zero 404s, and asserts every dictionary object key and decision id is reachable from site/index.html within three clicks; fail VERIFY when reachability or links break.

items.reason: Without `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md`, a layperson reading only `docs/` cannot produce executable artifacts for the five-step scenario. The decision register and dictionary coverage matrix exist but require expert reading. There is no doc explaining how to create an organization (no POST endpoint is documented — Supabase REST is read-only and Edge Functions only cover gradebook/audit/LTI/Caliper/OAuth-receipt), no documented flow for enrolling 10 students, no `/lineItems` POST documentation, no CASE alignment write path documented, and no Caliper-feed *read* endpoint exists (only ingestion).
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus runtime gaps: write the five-step guide and add the missing write endpoints (school/class create, enrollment write, gradebook line-item create, CASE alignment link, Caliper feed read) as Edge Functions or document explicitly which steps are simulated vs runnable.

items.reason: `scripts/evaluate_platform.py` defines `DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES` for every rubric id, `normalize_traced_cause()` ensures non-pass items always carry a non-empty `traced_cause` naming the lowest-layer artifact, and `normalize_buildability_gaps()` extracts per-step gaps with traced causes. The output schema documented at the top of the file requires `traced_cause` and `buildability_gaps[].traced_cause`.

VERIFY.md's last command is `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, run after each Codex iteration. The evaluator writes `site/api/platform-evaluation.json` with `done`, `counts`, `items` (each with status and traced_cause), and `buildability_gaps` per the schema docstring.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` defines `read_overrides()` to read `docs/loop-overrides.md` at iteration start, `overrides_paused()` to halt before the next iteration when `PAUSE` appears on its own line outside HTML comments, and `format_overrides_for_prompt()` injects the cleaned content under the prompt's 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md`' authoritative section. The pause check runs before `run_codex` for the next iteration.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` calls `run_evaluator()` and checks `if after_eval and after_eval.get('done'): print('Evaluator reports `done` after this iteration. Stopping the loop.'); return 0`. The pre-iteration check also returns early when the evaluator already reports done. There is no numeric-threshold gate; `--max-iterations` and `--stall-limit` are explicit safety rails.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented POST endpoint to create an organization. Supabase REST grants are SELECT-only on organizations; no Edge Function covers org creation, and docs/build-an-edtech-app.md does not exist.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus supabase/functions/create-organization/index.ts (or a documented direct DB seed path): add the write path and a curl example bound to a tenant JWT.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented endpoint for creating a class with one teacher and ten student enrollments. The hosted REST grants do not include INSERT on classes/enrollments, and no Edge Function wraps this.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: supabase/functions/create-class/index.ts and docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: add a JWT-forwarding Edge Function that inserts class+enrollment rows under tenant RLS, and document its curl invocation with dictionary-linked field names.

buildability_gaps.gap: gradebook-bulk-submit Edge Function exists for results, but creating a line_item first is not documented and not exposed as an Edge Function. The buildability scenario requires posting a numeric grade for a *new* assignment, which the docs do not cover end-to-end.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: supabase/functions/create-line-item/index.ts and docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: add line_item creation and document the line_item->result chain with curl and dictionary links to canonical.outcome.line_item and canonical.outcome.result.

buildability_gaps.gap: CASE alignment is documentation-only per DEC-012; there is no runtime endpoint documented for linking an assignment (line_item) to a CASE standard URI, and `dictionary/case-core.v1.json` lists no executable alignment write path.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json plus supabase/functions/link-case-standard/index.ts (or document the deferral explicitly in docs/build-an-edtech-app.md as a simulated step): add a CASE alignment write path or mark step (d) as out-of-scope with a documented stub example.

buildability_gaps.gap: caliper-event-ingestion Edge Function only writes receipts; no Edge Function or REST view exposes a class-scoped Caliper-style activity feed for reading. docs/lead-spec-accounting.md confirms 'Full raw-event persistence ... remains a future backend slice.'
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: supabase/functions/caliper-feed-read/index.ts plus a public.caliper_events table and view: add immutable raw-event storage, a tenant/class-scoped feed read function, and document its curl call in docs/build-an-edtech-app.md.

buildability_gaps.gap: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md does not exist, so a layperson has no single guide that strings the five steps together with copy-pasteable curl bound to dictionary anchors.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: create the end-to-end guide that links every field reference to its dictionary entry and every enum value to the shared_enum, against the live https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co runtime.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
